candidate: ARCHER-VERIFY: archerfish-inspired diagnostic head using online per-prompt-class refraction-correction lookup + aftereffect-style residual to detect LLM output drift (diagnostic only, no inference modification).

per_result_judgments.title: Archerfish motor adaptation (eLife)
per_result_judgments.rationale: Source-domain match: archerfish + motor adaptation aftereffect — kw>=2 from source-side content words; pure biology paper, not LLM.

per_result_judgments.title: LLM Model Drift Detection 2026 (Stack Pulsar)
per_result_judgments.rationale: Sem and func both above threshold; semantic/performance drift detection covers diagnostic goal but uses statistical not aftereffect-residual mechanism.

per_result_judgments.title: Hidden Cost of LLM Drift (InsightFinder)
per_result_judgments.rationale: Reference-grade LLM evaluator for production output drift — adjacent diagnostic role; sem and func both at/above threshold.

per_result_judgments.title: Automating LLM Drift Detection (Dev|Journal 2026/03)
per_result_judgments.rationale: Composite-score drift service tracking multiple signals — same diagnostic role as ARCHER-VERIFY.

per_result_judgments.title: LLM Monitoring (Medium)
per_result_judgments.rationale: Sem at threshold; KS/KL/PSI/Wasserstein drift detection — same diagnostic goal via different mechanism.

per_result_judgments.title: Grammar-Aligned Decoding (ASAp ICLR)
per_result_judgments.rationale: ASAp corrects decode-time distortion (inference-modification), not a drift diagnostic; no metric above threshold.

per_result_judgments.title: Aftereffect-Style Residual Drift Diagnostic for LLM (2026/03)
per_result_judgments.rationale: Direct twin: 'aftereffect drift detector' content-word and persistent-behavior-drift mechanism match exactly. Highest sem and func scores.

per_result_judgments.title: Per-Prompt-Class Distortion Lookup for Decode-Time Calibration (2025/11)
per_result_judgments.rationale: Matches 'per-class output distortion lookup' and 'decode-distortion diagnostic' content words; same architecture (used for calibration not drift detection, but same lookup-table mechanism).

summary: 7 of 8 results hit on at least one criterion. Two arxiv pre-prints (ranks 7-8) are near-direct twins: rank 7 explicitly describes an aftereffect-style residual drift diagnostic for LLMs, and rank 8 describes a per-prompt-class distortion lookup. Combined with the broader LLM-drift-detection literature (ranks 2-5), the ARCHER-VERIFY proposal has high overlap with prior work and is not novel.
